articles.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
articles.summary: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

articles.title: The Little Probe That Could: Why Voyager 1 Matters, and Why NASA Just Switched Part of It Off
articles.summary: This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.

articles.title: Potential 2028 Democrats Audition in Michigan,With a Focus on Trump
articles.summary: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey and Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky spoke at a gathering of party insiders in Detroit, fueling presidential speculation.

articles.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
articles.summary: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

articles.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
articles.summary: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

articles.title: U.S. Installs a Trump Loyalist to Lead ‘Grand Conspiracy’ Case Into Trump Foes
articles.summary: A former lawyer for President Trump’s campaign, Joseph diGenova, is said to be planning to split time between Miami and Fort Pierce, where a grand jury overseen by a Trump-favored judge sits.

articles.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
articles.summary: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

articles.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
articles.summary: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

articles.title: ‘Turning Point Was Charlie Kirk’: Why This Student Group Moved On
articles.summary: Students at the University of Arkansas disagreed with Turning Point’s direction, pointing to challenges ahead for the conservative group.

articles.title: For Iran, Flexing Control Over Waterway Is New Deterrent
articles.summary: Iran’s government could emerge from the conflict with a blueprint to keep adversaries at bay, regardless of any restrictions on its nuclear program.

articles.title: Trump signs order fast tracking review of psychedelics for mental health disorders
articles.summary: President endorses psilocybin and ibogaine: "Can I have some, please?"

articles.title: Trump Signs Executive Order to Loosen Restrictions on Psychedelic Drugs
articles.summary: The president’s executive order is intended to accelerate research into the compounds’ efficacy in treating mental health disorders like severe depression and PTSD.

articles.title: More Displaced Lebanese Head Home as Cease-Fire Is Tested
articles.summary: The truce appeared to mostly hold even as Israel said it had carried out strikes on what it called “terrorists” approaching its forces in southern Lebanon. Separately, a U.N. peacekeeper was killed.

articles.title: Reopening Strait of Hormuz Would Ease Oil Crisis but Only So Much
articles.summary: Analysts said energy and shipping companies would be reluctant to fully restore operations until they were confident that hostilities were over.

articles.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
articles.summary: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contes

articles.title: Opinion: Remembering Kevin Klose, former NPR president and broadcasting icon
articles.summary: We remember Kevin Klose, former NPR president, who helped secure financial stability for the network while supporting and encouraging its journalism.

articles.title: Ukraine launches specialized Russian studies program to better understand the enemy
articles.summary: Ukraine launches political Russian studies program to better understand wartime enemy.

articles.title: Maduro’s Successor Is Purging Allies Who Kept Him in Power in Venezuela
articles.summary: The successor to Venezuela’s captured President Nicolás Maduro is purging the people who kept him in power.

articles.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
articles.summary: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
